title: cerinthe major subsp. elegans
intro: Cerinthe major subsp. elegans, also known as Blue Skyflower, is a flowering plant species in the family Boraginaceae, characterized by its blue or purple flowers and hairy, gray-green leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, boasting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Cerinthe major subsp. elegans include its potential to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and support skin health due to its antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, where its essential oils and bioactive compounds can be extracted and utilized for therapeutic purposes.
